  • No proctoring: The exam experience is more flexible and accessible.
  • Smart retakes: If you fail a scenario and still have time left within the two-hour exam window, you can retake it immediately, and it still counts as a single exam attempt.
  • Pause functionality: You can pause the AI interaction if you need time to think, but be aware that the overall exam timer continues running. 
To help learners understand exactly what to expect, we created a short video that walks through the experience and shows how the AI role play works in practice.
 
A step toward future-ready certifications
The redesign of the SAP Analytics Cloud certification signals a broader transformation in professional learning and validation. As AI becomes a standard part of everyday workflows, certifications must evolve to assess judgment, problem-solving, and execution rather than isolated knowledge.
This move brings SAP certifications closer to how work is actually done and closer to how real-world skills should be measured.
It's time to start a new way of certification!

entry.title: A.I Assisted Transition at your own Pace: BW7.5 to BDC Pilot Experiences
entry.content:
This blog is part of a series related to a complex BW7.5 customer case where we explored together the opportunity to transition to BDC. You can find the intro blog below, plus the links to the subsequent blogs will be listed here -

  •  Link 1
  • Link 2 
  • Link 3

The Time is Now

At the time of typing this it is still early 2026. The year’s priorities and goals are being set. For many of our BW customers, the end of maintenance deadline set at the end of 2027 for NW 7.5 is looming large. Questions such as the below are being asked –

  • How can we maintain what we have built over many years without rebuilding from the ground up?
  • How can we make use of the latest and greatest from A.I?
  • Can we continue to use our existing skills?
  • Can we remain compliant to our regulatory requirements?

The goal of this series of blogs is to outline what customers on BW 7.5 can do to modernise their mission critical system of engagement at their own pace whilst embracing A.I, ensuring they avoid expensive regret cost migrations to third party platforms that can often spiral, seldom delivering on the initial promise. These blogs are based on first hand experiences in a pilot project for BW7.5 to BDC.

Interested to learn more? Read on

The Problem Statement

BW 7.5 is the last version of the ABAP stack SAP Business Warehouse that was initially released in the late 1990’s, it delivers out of the box capabilities (business content, extractor logic, authorisation concepts etc) to manage complex third normal form data structures from SAP ECC (legacy SAP application around finance, HR, supply chain etc) across a layered scalable architecture (LSA). In 2016, the ‘new’ BW was released called BW/4HANA with a roadmap to 2040+, triggering a timeline that will culminate in 2027 with the end of mainstream maintenace of the ABAP version BW7.5.

However, many customers for a variety of reasons chose not to move to BW4. Either they have too much logic built in objects not easily ported (for example APDs, BEx) or they were enticed by promises made by best of breed data vendors in cloud native data lake architectures to migrate their BW workload. Often these project spiral in costs and effort without delivering sufficient value.

The Solution

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), released in February 2025 is SAP’s fully managed SaaS solution built for modern workloads and to natively handle data-as-a-service from across the SAP Business Suite applications (S/4HANA Public and Private, SuccessFactors, Concur etc). This is achieved at the click of a button whereby ‘Data Products’ are loaded and kept in sync with backend transactional systems, removing the leg work for customers looking to get rapid value from their data. Business Content and Intelligent applications are installed via the BDC Cockpit, surfacing KPIs for decision making across a host of business areas, with a vast amount more to come in 2026. With BDC, SAP Databricks, SAP Snowflake and other best of breed solutions work in tandem with SAP Datasphere on the semantically rich data products providing customers a unified data architecture to deliver next generation A.I use cases.

What adds to the excitement of BW customers setting their new year goals? BDC is the go-to solution for SAP Business Warehouse. BDC provides a strong roadmap for customers to modernise their BW7.5 environment at their own pace, retaining their investments, their skills, keeping their data available and opening their rich sets of historic business data for A.I. This is achieved through the ‘Lift, Shift & Innovate’ approach that allows for large complex BWs to move to a managed cloud service (Private Cloud Edition) of BW within the formation of SAP Business Data Cloud.

The Lift phase moves existing SAP BW systems as-is to SAP-managed cloud infrastructure, extending maintenance timelines (2030 instead of 2027) and reducing operational overhead. The Shift phase exposes SAP BW data as Data Products using the Data Product Generator tool, enabling integration with SAP Datasphere and external data sources while preserving existing use cases. The Innovate phase explores SAP-managed Data Products and Intelligent Applications to replace legacy SAP BW scenarios with modern analytics, AI capabilities, and access to an open partner ecosystem. This phased approach mitigates risk by maintaining existing investments while providing a clear path to cloud-native analytics and reduced Total Cost of Ownership.

There are some prerequisites for your BW system to fulfil prior to embarking on the journey with BDC, the minimum version supporting the data product generator is SPS 24 and it needs to be running on HANA.

Many large global organisations reliant on BW for their strategic reporting, planning/business steering have already begun the transition to BDC to realise the benefits of modernisation to cloud native at their own pace.

‘Vibe Migration’

In Autumn/Winter 2025 the SAP EMEA and Global Solution Advisory teams worked cohesively on a pilot to demonstrate the BW to BDC move for a specific customer case.

This case assessed all aspects of a complex BW7.5 system after the Lift to PCE, demonstrating how the Shift and Innovate phases can look using the modern tools available in SAP BDC with a focus on SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud and Artificial Intelligence. Throughout the subsequent blog posts we will share the findings with specifics on how end of life (EoL) objects such as APDs, BEx and other scenarios can be covered either through automated migration to SAP BDC Datasphere, A.I assisted conversion or rebuild.

Please stay tuned for the next in this series of blog posts related to the Data Product Generator.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Planning with Live Snowflake Data
entry.content:
Introduction

In Q4 2025, we released live versions for planning models in SAP Analytics Cloud: 

  • In seamless planning, you can connect live to fact data in SAP Datasphere (blogpost)
  • In non-seamless planning scenarios, you can connect to the live SQL sources available in SAP Analytics Cloud. Up until now, Google BigQuery was the only available source (blogpost)

With QRC1 2026, Snowflake is added to the portfolio of live sources and with that, you can now reference Snowflake data for planning without replication!

 

How does it work?

In my blogpost on live versions from Google BigQuery, I demonstrated how to add live data to an existing model. So this time, we will take another approach and create a planning model from data.

  • Go to the modeler
  • Create model
  • Create model from data
  • Choose Snowflake. You can filter the list of connections to Data Storage “Cloud”
  • Choose a connection.
  • Choose a view
  • The model is created along the source view’s structures. You see the instant live connection in the data preview

Model creation from live data

You can now work with this model as you know from model’s without live versions. In the example below, we remove dimensions and measures that we do not need. We re-create one of the measures as a live calculation.

Calculations on live data

We can also leverage the data in data actions. See this example where we copy the Actual data into a plan version with a factor. The actual version that you see is sourced directly from Snowflake. When we copy the data into a plan version, the plan data is stored in the fact table of our model in SAP Analytics Cloud. 

Planning

 

FAQ

Where can I use this feature?

This feature is available for SAP Analytics Cloud models that are not deployed to SAP Datasphere (=not using seamless planning).

Can I use any source view?

Yes. However, not all data types are supported (details).

What can I do with live versions? (And what can I not do?)

  • You can display the live version data in the model data foundation and in tables, charts, etc. in stories.
  • You can reference the live version data in model and story calculations as well as data actions (incl. advanced formulas).
  • You cannot write back to the source. You can copy the live version data into planning versions via copy/paste in the table, data actions and version management.

How do I see if data is live or not?

  • In models, a live version is highlighted in the data preview. In the version dimension, you see the data source per version. 
  • In tables, live version data is locked for data entry and formatted accordingly. Reasons for unplannable data give accurate information.

 

Conclusion

Live data access in planning models represents a massive opportunity. SAP Analytics Cloud aligns with SAP Business Data Cloud’s ambition to be open and keeps on expanding its live connectivity portfolio. Look forward to use your Azure data for planning in the future and follow the respective innovation in the Roadmap Explorer.

entry.title: What's New on SAP Analytics Cloud Home Screen 2026 Q1 QRC: New Admin Cards
entry.content:
I'm excited to introduce three new Administrator Cards on the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Home screen in the Q1 QRC release. These cards give administrators a direct, customizable presence on users’ home screens and surface the operational insights needed to keep your SAC environment healthy and well governed with less time spent hunting for root causes.

What’s new

1 - Admin custom card

  • What it is: A fully customizable card that lets administrators display access links, announcements, and any other custom messages directly on users’ home screens.
  • How to create it: from the admin settings and under default appearance tab, click "+ Add Custom Card"

  •  What you can include: images, text, tags, and links tailored to the message or audience.

  • Important behavior: If an administrator locks and saves the custom card, users will not be able to move or hide it from their home screen.
  • Benefits: Deliver timely announcements (upgrades, maintenance windows, training links), promote key content or processes, and ensure critical guidance is seen by target audiences.

2- Administrative actions card

  • What it is: A focused card that summarizes critical recommendations for admins.
  • Purpose: Reduce time spent on root-cause analysis by surfacing key actionable items and prioritized recommendations.
  • Benefits: Enables faster, proactive housekeeping and maintenance helping admins resolve issues earlier and keep the system running smoothly.

 3 - System monitor card

  • What it is: A dedicated admin card that brings a concise, comprehensive summary of system health to the Home screen.
  • What it shows: critical system metrics, user/license health, performance hotspots, and governance-related recommendations.
  • Benefits: Empowers administrators to spot trends, identify performance issues quickly, and make informed decisions about scaling, licensing, or governance actions.

 How these cards help your organization

  • Improve visibility: Place critical notices and system health indicators where admins and users will see them daily.
  • Save time: The Administrative Actions card reduces investigation time by collecting high-priority recommendations in one place.
  • Encourage consistency: Locked custom cards ensure essential guidance (policies, access instructions, or emergency contacts) remains visible.
  • Drive governance: The System Monitor card makes it easier to track license usage, performance patterns, and governance recommendations without running separate reports.

Quick admin tips

  • Use images and tags on the custom card to increase clarity and engagement for announcements and links.
  • Lock and save a custom card only when you want fixed visibility for all users; otherwise let users personalize their Home experience.
  • Treat the Administrative Actions card as your operational to-do list, prioritize items and turn recurring recommendations into scheduled maintenance tasks.
  • Monitor the System Monitor card regularly to catch performance hotspots early and adjust configurations or scale resources as needed.

Getting started

These cards arrive with the Q1 QRC release. If you’re an admin, check your Home screen configuration options to enable and customize these cards. If you need to align messaging across teams, plan your card content and locking strategy before publishing.

entry.title: SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 + ESM - Creating a connection with SAP Analytics Cloud Enterprise [SAC]
entry.content:
SAP Sales & Service Cloud Version 2 (V2) and Enterprise Service Management (ESM) deliver modern, modular customer‑experience capabilities. But as organizations scale, the real differentiator becomes analytics—the ability to unify sales, service, and enterprise operations into a single insights layer.

SAP provides two major analytics options:

  1. Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud (eSAC) – included and built into V2 and ESM
  2. SAP Analytics Cloud Enterprise Edition – the full standalone analytics platform

Pre-requisities

  • SSO must be enabled in both systems (SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 & SAP Analytics Cloud)
  • Login via SSO in Sales and Service Cloud v2 Application.
  • Login to Enterprise SAC using the same SSO as used above.
  • Now try to create the connection and it should be successful (shown in below steps - screenshots).

Note : While doing handshake - Login to both system should be in same browser but in different tabs.

Step 1 : SSO must be enabled in order to work both Applications ( SSC v2 & SAC)

Step 2 : Login to SAP Analytics Cloud (Enterprise License version) and you must be Administrator to config

Step 3 : Select Connect to Live Data - Select a Data Source - SAP Cloud Application

Step 4 : Select from dropdown SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2

Step 5 : Enter the Application URL of your SAP Sales & Service Cloud Version 2 URL then immediately the Redirection happens to authenticate against both systems if the same IDP matches with successfull response.

 

Conclusion

Integrating SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 + ESM with SAP Analytics Cloud Enterprise unlocks powerful, cross-functional insights. While eSAC provides rapid, embedded visualization, SAC Enterprise enables enterprise-scale reporting and combines data from multiple systems.

For organizations seeking lighter setups, custom integration patterns using embedded Analytics within v2, using V2 REST APIs and BTP or SAP Databricks are viable alternatives.

By following the steps and architecture options outlined in this guide, your organization can create a unified analytics environment that empowers sales, service, and enterprise teams with real-time, actionable insights.
